scenarios.task: Our biggest customer ($180k/year renewal in 5 months) is demanding a custom report builder. Sales says we lose them without it. Engineering estimates 2 quarters for one team. Meanwhile our activation rate for new signups is 34% and we have a backlog item to fix onboarding. I'm the CEO — tell me what to do.
criteria: Asks for demand evidence beyond n=1 — whether other customers or prospects have asked for report building — before any build commitment
States the opportunity cost concretely: two quarters of the team is bought by not fixing 34% activation, and weighs those against each other
Proposes at least one specific smaller de-risking alternative: scoped export/API, services or partner workaround, or a phased commitment tied to the renewal
Recommends writing the decision down with explicit success criteria and kill criteria (or renewal-contingent terms)
Interrogates the threat itself — talk to the customer about the underlying job the report builder serves — rather than accepting sales' framing

scenarios.task: We shipped a template gallery 2 quarters ago to drive workflow creation. Adoption is 8%: measured as users who opened the gallery at least once. The team wants to invest another quarter adding more templates because 'it just needs more content.' Your call?
criteria: Explicitly rejects gallery-open as an adoption metric and names a value event (e.g. workflow created from a template) as the correct one
Treats 8% after two quarters as evidence the hypothesis failed, requiring diagnosis (funnel analysis or user research) before further investment
Asks what the original success target and kill criteria were when the feature shipped
Proposes a cheap targeted test of the 'more content' hypothesis instead of a full quarter of content production
Explicitly includes killing or repositioning the feature as live options

scenarios.task: Two features compete for next quarter, one team available. A: Slack notifications — sales cites 6 lost deals mentioning it, support gets ~30 requests/month, est. 6 weeks. B: AI-powered workflow suggestions — CEO is excited, no customer requests yet, est. 10 weeks with ML uncertainty. Walk me through the decision like a PM, numbers included.
criteria: Produces an actual scored comparison with numeric estimates (reach, impact, confidence, effort) for both features — adjective rankings do not satisfy this
Explicitly discounts B's score because its reach/impact inputs are invented while A has demand evidence (lost deals, ticket volume)
Handles CEO enthusiasm as an explicit input: proposes a timeboxed discovery spike for B or names it a strategic bet with its own budget — neither silently obeying nor ignoring
Incorporates the 6-week vs 10-week effort gap and ML delivery risk into the score or final call
Ends with an unambiguous pick plus the specific evidence that would reverse it
